channel.title: Chart Breakers & Star Stories
channel.itunes:author: Family TVR
channel.itunes:summary: Did your favorite artist just break a record or a heart? The charts are screaming, and we have the inside story you won't hear anywhere else.
channel.description: The latest music trends, chart updates, and star stories from Billboard and beyond. Stay updated with the Pop Culture.

item.title: ‘Saturday Night Live’: Where to Watch the Sketch Comedy Show Online for Free
item.description:
[Intro music plays]
Host: "Hey there, TV fans. Welcome to our latest podcast. This week, we're talking about the upcoming episode of Saturday Night Live, featuring host Colman Domingo and musical guest Anitta. The show airs live on NBC and Peacock Premium Plus on April 11th at 11:30 p.m. ET/8:30 p.m. PT. But don't worry if you don't have cable - we've got you covered. Let's dive into the ways you can watch SNL online for free."
[Short music transition]
Host: "First up, you can grab a free trial to DirecTV, which gets you access to NBC and a live feed of SNL without needing a cable subscription. The service starts at $59.99 for the first month, but you can sign up for a five-day free trial to watch for free. Alternatively, you can check out Sling TV, which also offers NBC and local channels, starting at $59.99 per month."
[Short music transition]
Host: "Another option is Peacock, the official NBC streamer. With the Peacock Premium Plus plan, you can watch SNL live for $16.99 per month. Although there's no free trial, you can sign up for a Walmart+ 30-day free trial to watch the episode for free. And if you're looking for a more extensive streaming library, you can consider Hulu + Live TV, which starts at $89.99 per month and gives you access to hundreds of live TV channels, including NBC."
[Outro music plays]
Host: "That's it for today's podcast. Colman Domingo and Anitta are set to bring the laughs and music to SNL on April 11th. Catch the episode live on NBC, Peacock Premium Plus, or through one of the streaming options we mentioned. Thanks for tuning in, and we'll see you next time."
